title: Aehr Test Systems Reports Fiscal 2026 First Quarter Financial Results
text: Aehr Test Systems announced fiscal 2026 Q1 results showing mixed performance. Net revenue reached $11.0 million, down from $13.1 million in the prior year quarter. GAAP net loss totaled $2.1 million, or $0.07 per diluted share, versus net income of $0.7 million in the same period last year. Non-GAAP results showed $0.2 million, or $0.01 per share, compared to $2.2 million the previous year. Bookings totaled $11.4 million for the quarter, with backlog standing at $15.5 million as of August 29, 2025. Effective backlog including recent bookings reached $17.5 million. Cash reserves were $24.7 million. CEO Gayn Erickson highlighted momentum in AI processor testing, stating the company was pleased with the start to the fiscal year with strong activity in Sonoma packaged-part burn-in systems and wafer-level testing solutions. The company emphasized significant customer wins, expanded product capabilities including 2000W power-per-device configurations, and strategic partnerships with major outsourced assembly and test providers. Management noted optimism across multiple markets while cautioning about tariff-related uncertainties.
